audit_item:
• Remove Nozzle Tip with mesh screen and run under warm water.
     • To remove Nozzle Tip for cleaning, cover the Sensor area with
        your finger to prevent dispensing of soap. Turn Nozzle to the left to
        loosen. Rinse Nozzle under hot water to loosen any soap residue and
        replace. Turn Nozzle to the right to tighten
• Place hand under Nozzle Tip to be sure soap is properly dispensed
• Gently wipe the dispenser with a cloth moistened with warm water and a diluted
neutral detergent
• Clean area between Lid and Spout to keep Lid swing free of soap residue
• Flush the system when minimal amount of soap is left in the bottle
       • Open the top-fill door using the BobKey, rotate door clockwise
       • Fill the dispenser with warm water.
       • Press the Automatic Flush Button located on the bottom housing assembly, just
          above the Partition Control Knob.
       • Once unit is flushed, Press the Automatic Flush Button again to stop dispensing.
       • Fill unit with fresh soap.
• Place hand under Nozzle Tip to be sure soap is properly dispensed.

I found this information online (Advisory Bulletin). https://www.bobrick.com/wp-content/uploads/TB-99.pdf

NOTES:
Please be concerned with the traffic on the sink usage, PM will take about ten to twenty mins for each unit. Depending on the soap amount inside the container.
If possible, perform the PM partially to allow the traffic to use the other sink. 
To save soap, it's best to perform the PM when the soap is at low level. Maybe early morning or late afternoon.
Check the condition of the office chair arm pads. This includes all of the chairs inside of the conference, training and offices. Replace the arm pads as needed.

This includes all chairs in the building (open office, conference, office, training, lunch room, etc). 
ALL CHAIRS:
Check to make sure all hardware is tight 
Check to see if there are signs of any cracking on the structural frame
Check to make sure chairs are level (no rocking back and forth)
Check cleanliness (stains, tears, etc)
ROLLING CHAIRS:
Clean and check casters as needed
Make sure cylinder is working
Check the tilt and other functions
BREAK ROOM CHAIRS:
Check the chairs feet (they are level and they have glides)
Look for cracks or deforming

audit_item:
*Use a bucket and dump all the ice out of the ice chute .
*Turn off the water to the machine under sink
* Remove top and front panel from the machine 
*Turn the power off to the machine Switch is in the middle .
*Disconnect hoses and water metering rod (Carefully) its Glass get the drain hose and remove the cap and let all the water run down to the drain.
*Now remove the water container and clean it with scale remover from Scotsman rinse it free of chemicals and place back  .
*Wipe drum and all around the ice drum on top free of deposits  .
*Turn water back on and let it run to rise out the cleaner then cap drain hose and turn power back on .
*Place all panels back on and run water and ice chute to free it from the scale remover .

Check lubrication of the following ,
*Refilled oil reservoir .
*Automatic lubricator of X Y Z axis guide rails .
*Wipe down and lubricate  guide rails for spindle .
*Lubricate vacuum pump
*Lubricate blower 
*Check all limit switches (There should be no resistance when moving each axis)
*Remove and blow out all 3 vacuum filters .
*Check tool touch off for loose hardware .
*Press the spindle tool button and use hand tool to clean inside the spindle after cleaning the spindle and the tool carousel needs to be referenced use trouble shooting notes .
*Check vacuum gauge  remove both sides of the hose to the gauge and the other end going to the pumps and blow it off till clean air comes out on both ends this needs to be done monthly    .

Miguel Diaz - 01/06/24 10:40:29 AM: Visually inspect the transporter motor for leaks 
-Check transporter chain oiler reservoir  for fluid level refill if needed with recommended lubricant mixture of 5:1 ratio of 140 WT gear oil and Tufoil- Voorwood part # 61504 or direct 1800 922-0075
-Check transporter chain for tension adjust if needed 
-Visually check shaper ball screws and slide rods for grease .Use TriGel -450 R ball screw lubricator and for the slides rods wipe with Acetone  move back and forth to clean .
-Remove build up debris from the shaper housing use air nozzle .
-Visually check the lower transporter feed chain tension .Proper tension is 1/2 to 1'' above the bottom edge of the electrical component area so that the chain does not show below this area .
Adjust if needed.

audit_item:
To stay in compliance with AQMD, we need to record all run hours in the generator logbook. Since the generator runs every Monday morning at 7am for 15 minutes, we will need to start logging that as maintenance and testing.
•	Every Monday after 7:15 AM make a new log entry in the binder inside the generator.
•	Take the start time from the previous weeks end time.
•	Fill out the rest of the sections that are outlined on the log sheet and make a comment that the reason the generator is running is due to maintenance and testing.
•	Check the fuel level of the generator. If it is below 50% full, schedule for a refuel.
Please note that if there is more than a 15-minute discrepancy between last weeks end time and the current weeks end time, that indicates that the generator ran outside its normal weekly run. In that case, please notify your supervisor.
Call Collicutt for a yearly service @ 1-562 944-4413 to be done by an Outside Mechanic .

2. “Dummy” test is in place and working properly (should be tested/verified).
3. VMP (Visual Manufacturing Procedure) is up to date and being used and followed by employees.
4. Procedure for Testing equipment is up to date (may be within the VMP).
5. Ensure mfg. is recording and logging all information on units tested.
6. Ensure mfg. is following the Line Reject Procedure.
   a. Following SP when they have to reject a dryer or cabinet, etc.  to ensure it is not mixed with good product.
7. Are there any safety relevant complaints?
8. Are there any changes in construction or components?
9. Is the final test performed according to the actual requirements of VDE (PM332e)?
10. Do workers have readily available up-to-date documents (manufacturing and test instructions and so on)?
11. Are the personnel carrying out assembly, final tests, and inspection of incoming goods trained?

TESTER MAINTERNANCE TO BE PERFORMED EVERY 4 MONTHS

Dis-assemble and clean Fastest fixture at that position, replace if needed.

General Maintenance
1) Clean and inspect carriage and guide.
2) Check side fence for alignment.

4) Inspect table top plates for wear.
5) Grease all bearings 21 total.
6) Clean side location sensor

audit_item:
*Remove and clean cabinet filters x 2 
*Check belt tension 
-Check for proper operation and lubrication  of transport chain 
*Blow down machine outside and inside 
-blow edge magazine stations and glue pot area Note:  away from the glue pot funnel 
-blow down all stations key areas where it moves guides ,bearings 
-blow down pressure zone rollers /Wipe them free of glue using rag and acetone /Lube main pressure roller gears with mobil blue grease .
*Check for proper operating pressures 
-make sure all regulators PSI are at the correct setting as the label says it should be 
*Clean snipping unit /Trimming with air ,vacuum and cloth only 
-Check and lube linear guides  for all machine /Grease 4 nipples from pre-mill remove cover to access fittings
-Lube all four rounding stations pendulum arms total of one grease fitting per unit for a total of 4 .
-Check shock absorbers 
-Check rockers 
*Check both oilers for proper levels refill if needed 
*Wipe and lubricate all moving parts inside the machine liner rails guide shafts  (Use air lube )
*Check and clean extraction hoods 
- dust collection openings 
-air suction openings on hot air blower 
*Check and By pass air valves
- check for leaks-
- check functions and replace if needed 
*Check for proper operation of the tracing rollers/ Guides  make sure all are spinning  wipe with scotch brite Remove any acuumulations from guide scrapper called stripping device 
*Check the blowing nozzles  that they are functioning correctly 
-Check  blades for the scrapers check for wear and or damage replace if needed 
*Clean guide shafts for the buffing units 
-Remove remaining glue and chips 
*Check hot air blowers visually check air intake opening and nozzle it must be clean .
-If cleaning is needed remove heat insulation ,loosen screws ,stretch nozzle of heater element tube and clean air intake opening and nozzle with vacuum cleaner put back like it was after cleaning

audit_item:
*Remove and clean cabinet filters x 2 
*Check belt tension 
-Check for proper operation and lubrication  of transport chain 
*Blow down machine outside and inside 
-blow edge magazine stations and glue pot area Note:  away from the glue pot funnel 
-blow down all stations key areas where it moves guides ,bearings 
-blow down pressure zone rollers /Wipe them free of glue using rag and acetone /Lube main pressure roller gears with mobil blue grease .
*Check for proper operating pressures 
-make sure all regulators PSI are at the correct setting as the label says it should be 
*Clean snipping unit /Trimming with air ,vacuum and cloth only 
-Check and lube linear guides  for all machine /Grease 4 nipples from pre-mill remove cover to access fittings
-Lube all four rounding stations pendulum arms total of one grease fitting per unit for a total of 4 .
-Check shock absorbers 
-Check rockers 
*Check both oilers for proper levels refill if needed 
*Wipe and lubricate all moving parts inside the machine liner rails guide shafts  (Use air lube )
*Check and clean extraction hoods 
- dust collection openings 
-air suction openings on hot air blower 
*Check and By pass air valves
- check for leaks-
- check functions and replace if needed 
*Check for proper operation of the tracing rollers/ Guides  make sure all are spinning  wipe with scotch brite Remove any acuumulations from guide scrapper called stripping device 
*Check the blowing nozzles  that they are functioning correctly 
-Check  blades for the scrapers check for wear and or damage replace if needed 
*Clean guide shafts for the buffing units 
-Remove remaining glue and chips 
*Check hot air blowers visually check air intake opening and nozzle it must be clean .
-If cleaning is needed remove heat insulation ,loosen screws ,stretch nozzle of heater element tube and clean air intake opening and nozzle with va

audit_item:

*Clean the general area free of glue use mineral sprits/acetone  if needed   .

audit_item:
*Remove and clean cabinet filters x 2 
*Check belt tension 
-Check for proper operation and lubrication  of transport chain 
*Blow down machine outside and inside 
-blow edge magazine stations and glue pot area Note:  away from the glue pot funnel 
-blow down all stations key areas where it moves guides ,bearings 
-blow down pressure zone rollers /Wipe them free of glue using rag and acetone /Lube main pressure roller gears with mobil blue grease .
*Check for proper operating pressures 
-make sure all regulators PSI are at the correct setting as the label says it should be 
*Clean snipping unit /Trimming with air ,vacuum and cloth only 
-Check and lube linear guides  for all machine /Grease 4 nipples from pre-mill remove cover to access fittings
-Lube all four rounding stations pendulum arms total of one grease fitting per unit for a total of 4 .
-Check shock absorbers 
-Check rockers 
*Check both oilers for proper levels refill if needed 
*Wipe and lubricate all moving parts inside the machine liner rails guide shafts  (Use air lube )
*Check and clean extraction hoods 
- dust collection openings 
-air suction openings on hot air blower 
*Check and By pass air valves
- check for leaks-
- check functions and replace if needed

*Check the blowing nozzles  that they are functioning correctly 
-Check  blades for the scrapers check for wear and or damage replace if needed 
*Clean guide shafts for the buffing units 
-Remove remaining glue and chips 
*Check hot air blowers visually check air intake opening and nozzle it must be clean .

audit_item:
-Visually inspect the transporter motor for leaks 
-Check transporter chain oiler reservoir  for fluid level refill if needed with recommended lubricant mixture of 5:1 ratio of 140 WT gear oil and Tufoil- Voorwood part # 61504 or direct 1800 922-0075
-Check transporter chain for tension adjust if needed 
-Visually check shaper ball screws and slide rods for grease .Use TriGel -450 R ball screw lubricator and for the slides rods wipe with Acetone  move back and forth to clean .
-Remove build up debris from the shaper housing use air nozzle .
-Visually check the lower transporter feed chain tension .Proper tension is 1/2 to 1'' above the bottom edge of the electrical component area so that the chain does not show below this area .
Adjust if needed.

audit_item:
*Remove each tool holder by pressing the red button this will release the tool .
-Do one at a time remove tool and blow out and clean bottom of tool  holder and collet with acetone on a ear swab .
Do not use any lubricant .

-Visually inspect the transporter motor for leaks 
-Check transporter chain oiler reservoir  for fluid level refill if needed with recommended lubricant mixture of 5:1 ratio of 140 WT gear oil and Tufoil- Voorwood part # 61504 or direct 1800 922-0075
-Check transporter chain for tension adjust if needed 
-Visually check shaper ball screws and slide rods for grease .Use TriGel -450 R ball screw lubricator and for the slides rods wipe with Acetone  move back and forth to clean .
-Remove build up debris from the shaper housing use air nozzle .
-Visually check the lower transporter feed chain tension .Proper tension is 1/2 to 1'' above the bottom edge of the electrical component area so that the chain does not show below this area .
Adjust if needed.

*Check lubrication of the following ,
*Automatic lubricator of X Y Z axis guide rails .
*Wipe down and lubricate  guide rails for spindle .
*Lubricate vacuum pump
*Lubricate blower 
*Refill oil reservoir with Vactra oil #2 to max 
*Check air lube reservoir located behind machine close bottom refill with air lube if needed 
*Check all limit switches (There should be no resistance when moving each axis)
*Remove and blow out all 3 vacuum filters .
*Check tool touch off for loose hardware .
*Press the spindle tool button and use hand tool to clean inside the spindle after cleaning the spindle and the tool carousel needs to be referenced use trouble shooting notes .
*Check vacuum gauge  remove both sides of the hose to the gauge and the other end going to the pumps and blow it off till clean air comes out on both ends this needs to be done monthly    .

Grease all 19 grease fittings
Grease ball screw nut for X Y Z axis .
Grease ball screw hold bearings 
Check all bolts and tighten levelers
Clean and check the spindle brushes .Note if a brush is less than  10 MM (0.4'') please change for new one .
Replace 1/2 SYOZ 25 COLLET if needed .

General Maintenance 
1)Check the variable speed motor and gear box oil levels.
2)Lubrication of all moving elements and rack support units.
3) Inspect feed belt tension guides and sprockets.
4) Vac. out control cabinets and clean fan filters.
5) Inspect all trim and cutter motors.
6) Inspect top roller guide wheels for proper operation.
7)Lube with air lube both sides of the feed track on the bottom .
8)Use tooth brush and brush the rod for the automatic buffer 
9)Spray the rod with LPS 1 make sure auto function is working properly.

audit_item:
1) Lubricate Slide,
2) Check alignment of the side and back rails.
3) Check sensors for proper read.
4) Vac. out electrical cabinets.
5) Check dust collection hose for proper operation.
6) Inspect and clean fan filters.
comments:
Gabriel Shinobi - 04/24/25  7:45:01 AM: Stiles tech found that the main drive sprocket was worn out, as well as the idol sprocket. Recommends replacing both along with the pins on the chain as they are worn as well. Waiting on quote from stiles on parts, will order with expedited shipping Will need to call to have techs come in to install,(possibly 1-2 days worth of work) will keep work order open, machine is currently ok to use, tech recommends the hold down not be adjusted down to minimize pressure until parts arrive. 

parts recommended
D1102004 joint pins (need to count quantity for full replacement)
D0301045 needle bearings 15-16HK
D1102002 ring tensioner wheel x2
C0301013  bearing 6207 2RS (35-72-17) x2
D1102095 steel towing gears (preassembled) x1
Z3581003 transmission shaft x1

General Maintenance
1) Check overall condition of machine.
2) Check table slide feed bearings for free rolling and free movement of fence.
3) Check blade condition for heavy wear and missing teeth.
4) Check condition of all jig guides.
5) Check condition of drive belts replace if cracked.
6) Check working condition of spindle brake cable.
7) Check all switch's for proper operation.
8) Inspect top feed roller wheels and grease wheels.
9) Make sure all safety guards are in place.

*Inspect bearing brackets all should ride firmly on frame slide bars .
*Check parallel adjustments on all four corners of the rollers .Note remove side chain to adjust using feeler gauge 000.20.
*Re-calibrate pressure roll opening indicator after adjusting corners.
*Check all fasteners for tightness in heater assembly and basic machine frame including electrical terminals. 
*Check for stuck debris on rollers and heaters remove any .
*Check main sprocket and key hole tighten set screws .
*Inspect rollers rubber surface .
*Lubricate machine (Chain, Gears ,Elevator and pivot points, idler studs gearbox shafts ETC ...)
Use High grade bearings grease for all grease fittings.
 Use SAE-40 machine oil for chains.
*Check drive chain tension leave slack per techs instructions
**After all of the above is done turn ON the heaters wait till it reaches temp (1050 F)and use the temp gun make sure all heaters go above 740 F .

Miguel Diaz - 11/07/22  7:42:26 AM: did the following 
General Maintenance 
1)Checked  the variable speed motor and gear box oil levels.
2)Lubrication of all moving elements and rack support units.
3) Inspect feed belt tension guides and sprockets.
4) Vac. out control cabinets and clean fan filters.
5) Inspect all trim and cutter motors.
6) Inspect top roller guide wheels for proper operation.
7)Check front guide bolts tighten if needed make sure front guide is not touching the feed belt causing vibration when passing the pieces .
8)Check green side table to be level with Machine feed belt .
8)Check side feeler guides for dry glue remove if any 
9)Use tooth brush and brush the rod for the automatic buffer 
10)Spray the rod with LPS 1 make sure auto function is working properly.
11)Raise beam height lock machine down and wet a cloth and wipe bottom feed track and top rollers .
12)Clean return system orange transfer bands with wet cloth while running on slow .

*Roller tables make sure no tools are stuck in between the rollers blow down machine check for stuck rollers replace if needed .

comments:
Miguel Diaz - 02/11/25 11:09:30 AM: General Maintenance 
1)Check the variable speed motor and gear box oil levels.
2)Lubrication of all moving elements and rack support units.
3) Inspect feed belt tension guides and sprockets.
4) Vac. out control cabinets and clean fan filters.
5) Inspect all trim and cutter motors.
6) Inspect top roller guide wheels for proper operation.
7)Lube with air lube both sides of the feed track on the bottom .
8)Use tooth brush and brush the rod for the automatic buffer 
9)Spray the rod with LPS 1 make sure auto function is working properly.

*Use a feeler gauge and close the trays tight against the rollers.Place the feeler in between the rollers and  tray to see if they are straight check both ends and the middle ..
*Please note if trays are not straight the glue will be  not spread evenly.

*Check that the safety interlock for the yellow doors are not loose tighten nut and bolt for both sides .

*Check the condition of the 4 bearings in the roller supports .
Grease them through the specially-Designed lubricators .

Location -Top part of the scissor lift mechanism
Use Reference pictures notes from Manual
*Check black tooth belt for damage check tension replace or adjust if needed .
*Check all guide rollers on X  and Y axis check for play check that all are moving and clean guides.
*Check sliding blocks for damage replace if needed
*Inspect power chain blow if needed 
*Check Transmission gears for leaks check oil level 
*Clean dust deposits from dive on transmission
*Grease the toothed segments with Molykote lube 
*Grease all bearing  nipples using Part #4-155-70-0005 make sure to grease until a fresh rim of grease forms at the seals .
*Grease the scissor lift mechanism  bearings 
*Grease rotary drive bearings 
*Check both drive tires make sure they are in the center and not drifting to the side look at the shaft no grease swirl marks should be seen tighten locks if needed

*Check all vacuum suction cups for leaks and proper function check for tears replace if needed .
*Clean Vacuum suction cups with soft cloth blow out with compressed air

Lower part of the machine -Location suction rail 
Use reference pictures notes from Manual 
*Check function of cylinders and valves also check if damaged or leaks 
*Check hoses and hose joints for leaks or damage 
*Clean or replace filter inserts if needed 
*Check Operating pressure 
*Check Limit switch for proper function 
*Clean light barriers with rag no solvents
*Clean all sensors free of dust 
*Check Vacuum and compressed air for supply and leaks 
*Check seals for damage replace if necessary 
*Check tooth belts for damage replace if worn 
*Check power cable chain for damage 
*Check guide rails Clean with cloth inspect for damage 
*Grease all ball rail guides lubricate with syringe until lubricant escapes .(Part#4-017-08-0073
*Grease central lubrication of guide carriage use grease gun 
*Remove and clean filters from control use compressed air 
*Check both L brackets in the middle to be leveled if they are bent upwards level back down 
*Check all blue collar bushings spray them with Teflon spray make sure all support arms are in place and retaining clips are in place .

*Remove and clean all venture on the bottom of the TLF .
*Replaced any filter elements if needed .

*Check all vacuum suction cups for leaks and proper function check for tears replace if needed .
*Clean Vacuum suction cups with soft cloth blow out with compressed air

1) Check overall condition of machine.
2) Check table slide feed bearings for free rolling and free movement of fence.
3) Check blade condition for heavy wear and missing teeth.
4) Check condition of all jig guides.
5) Check condition of drive belts replace if cracked.
6) Check working condition of spindle brake cable.
7) Check all switch's for proper operation.
8) Inspect top feed roller wheels and grease wheels.
9) Make sure all safety guards are in place.

3) Inspect slide for damage or wear.

7)Check that all sensors are working properly

9)Check all back stop sensors total of 6  they should only turn ON when touching  should not turn on no more than 1/16 away.

General Maintenance 
1)Check the variable speed motor and gear box oil levels.
2)Lubrication of all moving elements and rack support units.
3) Inspect feed belt tension guides and sprockets.
4) Vac. out control cabinets and clean fan filters.
5) Inspect all trim and cutter motors.
6) Inspect top roller guide wheels for proper operation.
7)Check front guide bolts tighten if needed make sure front guide is not touching the feed belt causing vibration when passing the pieces .
8)Check green side table to be level with Machine feed belt .
8)Check side feeler guides for dry glue remove if any 
9)Use tooth brush and brush the rod for the automatic buffer 
10)Spray the rod with LPS 1 make sure auto function is working properly.
11)Raise beam height lock machine down and wet a cloth and wipe bottom feed track and top rollers .
12)Clean return system orange transfer bands with wet cloth while running on slow .

audit_item: SCMH to perform Scheduled - PM Service -Planned Maintenance. ----
comments:
Miguel Diaz - 12/06/22  1:32:06 PM: Checked function of cylinders and valves also check if damaged or leaks 
*Checked hoses and hose joints for leaks or damage 
*Cleaned or replace filter inserts if needed 
*Checked Operating pressure 
*Checked Limit switch for proper function 
*Cleaned light barriers with rag no solvents
*Cleaned all sensors free of dust 
*Checked Vacuum and compressed air for supply and leaks 
*Checked seals for damage replace if necessary 
*Checked tooth belts for damage replace if worn 
*Check power cable chain for damage 
*Check guide rails Clean with cloth inspect for damage 
*Greased all ball rail guides lubricate with syringe until lubricant escapes .(Part#4-017-08-0073
*Greased central lubrication of guide carriage use grease gun 
*Remove and clean filters from control use compressed air 
*Check both L brackets in the middle to be leveled if they are bent upwards level back down 
*Checked all blue collar bushings spray them with Teflon spray make sure all support arms are in place and retaining clips are in place .
